operator_scope.semantic_signature: kernel-authoring legality for fused pointwise tasks

valid_when: Experiment mode is kernel-authoring mode and the submitted candidate path must implement the target pointwise semantics in a custom CUDA/Triton/custom kernel.
invalid_when: A separate library-composition mode explicitly permits high-level PyTorch/ATen composition and reports it separately.
preconditions: Check task-specific banned API list before generating candidate code.

mechanism: Calling high-level PyTorch or torch.nn.functional activation/composition APIs in the submitted path delegates the target semantics to the framework and invalidates kernel-authoring evaluation.

instructions: Do not call torch.nn.functional.gelu, torch.nn.functional.silu, torch.gelu, torch.silu, torch.clamp, torch.add, torch.mul, or equivalent ATen/PyTorch high-level ops to complete the target pointwise expression in the submitted path.
Allowed host-side actions are limited to metadata reads, output allocation, and launching the custom low-level kernel unless the task spec says otherwise.
If a PyTorch call is used only to allocate or inspect metadata, keep it outside the target semantic path and make the boundary explicit for legality review.
action.patch_hint: Move activation and affine arithmetic into the Triton/CUDA kernel body; keep Python wrapper to shape checks, output allocation, and launch.

expected_effect.correctness: Does not change numerical semantics when the low-level kernel is correct.
expected_effect.performance: Prevents illegal fast paths from being counted as legal performance results.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Avoids wasting iterations on candidates that will be rejected by legality review.
expected_effect.risk: Static string filters alone are insufficient; dynamic trace or manual review may still be required.

correctness_matrix: candidate path trace contains no target high-level fallback
wrapper only allocates output and launches custom kernel
validation.promotion_policy: Stable legality rule for this repository protocol; not a hardware performance rule.

known_failures: A candidate can pass correctness by delegating GELU/SILU/clamp/affine work to PyTorch, but is illegal under kernel-authoring mode.
Using contiguous(), reshape(), permute(), or as_strided() to trigger the target data movement may require review.
boundary_tests: static scan for torch.nn.functional and torch target ops
dynamic trace of submitted candidate path
do_not_apply: secondary library-composition mode with separate reporting

portable_part: kernel-authoring separation of low-level implementation from high-level target fallback
replace_on_new_backend: backend-specific list of low-level kernel launch and allocation APIs
